summary: PVF dispatchers still resolve sender-chosen handler strings with Call Compile; init already precompiles SRVFNC*/CLTFNC* globals, so a validated missionNamespace lookup can close the dispatch trust boundary without changing the high-level protocol.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus dispatchers to use allowlisted missionNamespace getVariable lookup, then smoke valid server/client commands plus forged invalid command rejection.

summary: The client Tactical menu gates ICBM locally, but a forged RequestSpecial payload can reach the server ICBM branch and spawn NukeDammage without server-side commander/upgrade/funds validation.
nextAction: Implement the ICBM playbook server-side: re-derive side/team/commander, validate upgrade/module/funds/object state, debit server-side and reject malformed payloads.

summary: Side-supply mutation is a direct temp publicVariable path for west/east; overspend can become a windfall. Resistance/GUER supply is partially scaffolded but not wired as a live economy side.
nextAction: Clamp negative west/east supply floors, validate temp-channel side/amount, and keep resistance economy disabled/documented until a resistance temp handler and three-way smoke exist.

summary: ATTACK_WAVE_INIT bypasses PVF and trusts client-supplied supply/side, allowing forged discounts that can drive side-wide unit prices to zero or negative.
nextAction: Treat ATTACK_WAVE_INIT as a request, re-derive server side supply, validate requester/side, clamp modifier/duration and preserve documented JIP behavior.

summary: Construction, defense, upgrades, player buys, structure sale, gear/EASA/service, supply rewards and WASP HQ recovery repeat the same client- or payload-authoritative spend/effect pattern.
nextAction: Do not patch these as isolated UI bugs. Pick a server-ledger/request model, then migrate flows in roadmap order with dedicated smoke records.

summary: Current source still has DR-33 factory queue issues: low-entropy random FIFO token and an empty-vehicle exit before the normal local queue-counter decrement.
nextAction: Patch local counter cleanup and token identity in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla with LoadoutManager, smoke repeated crewless/crewed buys and leave queu broadcast reduction as a separate UI-aware follow-up.

summary: Supply missions still trust client-stamped truck variables and have cooldown/idempotency work pending; the command-center nearestObjects scan is patched in source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla Takistan, smoke pending.
nextAction: Finish cooldown casing, duplicate-start guards, disconnect cleanup, reward/source authority and Arma smoke before merging supply-heli work into baseline.

summary: Source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla Takistan now register HandleParatrooperMarkerCreation and ship the handler; Arma smoke and modded handler drift cleanup are still pending.
nextAction: Smoke paratrooper drop marker creation and decide whether divergent modded folders should copy the handler or drop the registration.

summary: MASH tents may still work for respawn, but MASH marker sync is not proven live: server relay code exists, the client receiver compile is commented, and no live client broadcast of the creation channel was found.
nextAction: Either revive with server-held marker list, unique names and JIP replay, or remove the dead relay/receiver path intentionally.

summary: Source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla Takistan remove the duplicate Skill_Init.sqf call; Skill_Init remains non-idempotent if future paths call it more than once, and Arma smoke is pending.
nextAction: Smoke Soldier/non-Soldier AI caps and consider guarding Skill_Init if new paths can call it again.

summary: Source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla Takistan FPS publishers now exit on non-dedicated hosts before their loops; dedicated/hosted smoke remains pending.
nextAction: Smoke dedicated RHUD/server FPS publishing plus hosted/listen no-spin behavior; defer publisher consolidation.

summary: WASP marker naming still has a short sleepless display wait in global_marking_monitor.sqf; this is a small performance cleanup, not a completed patch.
nextAction: Add tiny sleep/backoff in source, propagate generated targets, and smoke map double-click/Enter/Escape marker naming behavior.

summary: Dialog/title resource IDD reuse is confirmed, including EASA/Economy sharing 23000 and title resources sharing 10200; normal dialog flow mitigates some risk, but future findDisplay/debug/control lookups can hit the wrong display.
nextAction: Assign unique display/title IDs, audit findDisplay assumptions, then smoke EASA, Economy, RscOverlay and OptionsAvailable flows.

summary: Client_UI_Gear_SaveTemplateProfile.sqf saves profile templates after buy-gear changes, but its weapon/magazine/backpack upgrade checks reference undefined _u_upgrade instead of an item or template upgrade value.
nextAction: Replace _u_upgrade in SaveTemplateProfile with item-upgrade or template-upgrade comparisons, then smoke allowed/locked template persistence and confirm no RPT undefined-variable error.

summary: Common_EquipVehicle.sqf and Common_EquipBackpack.sqf apply cargo with inclusive loops from 0 to count(_items), causing one out-of-range select attempt per non-empty cargo group.
nextAction: Change all five equip loops to count(_items)-1 in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla with LoadoutManager, and smoke vehicle/backpack cargo application plus empty cargo groups for no RPT out-of-range errors.

summary: Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf treats the whole payload as _side while also selecting commander from element 1; RequestNewCommander sends [_side,_assigned_commander]. Assignment likely lands in the caller, but helper-side AI shutdown and notification routing use the bad side value.
nextAction: Patch _side to select 0 in the helper, remove the duplicate new-commander-assigned notification from caller or helper intentionally, then smoke commander reassignment/vote fallback and generated propagation.

summary: AI commander state/funds and upgrade worker exist, but no audited source owner starts the autonomous commander loop, schedules upgrades, drives AIBuyUnit or uses the move interval; old AI supply trucks are config-gated broken.
nextAction: Owner should choose minimal guard/remove versus full revival before autonomous AI supply-heli/logistics work; player-run PR #1 supply heli should remain separate.

summary: GUI_Menu_Service.sqf enables service buttons from cached price variables before recalculating prices, and action branches do not consistently re-check price, funds and usable vehicle state before debiting/spawning support scripts.
nextAction: Add action-time price, funds and usable-context guards before service debits/spawns in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, and smoke affordable/unaffordable rearm/refuel/repair/heal without claiming server authority.

summary: Current tracked source fixes are in the canonical propagation ledger: paratrooper marker registration, client skill init idempotency, hosted server FPS loop exits, supply mission scan narrowing and supply player-list indexing are now present in Chernarus + maintained Vanilla, with Arma smoke pending.
nextAction: Run/record paratrooper, skill, FPS, supply mission and reconnect/JIP smoke gates before claiming release-complete.

summary: Integration trust boundaries now have one canonical audit: DiscordBot JSON intake is the active TypeNameHandling.All risk; the in-repo extension writer uses TypeNameHandling.None; AntiStack uses a separate absent A2WaspDatabase extension; shipped BattlEye is AFK-only.
nextAction: Harden DiscordBot JSON intake first, then extension write observability, AntiStack missing-DLL/shape guards and owner-confirmed BattlEye deployment posture.

summary: AntiStack now has a canonical runtime/procedure audit. WFBE_C_ANTISTACK_ENABLED defaults on and disabled-mode guards exist, but enabled-mode callDatabase wrappers still compile A2WaspDatabase return strings and assume array shapes.
nextAction: Add a central safe response validator/circuit breaker for A2WaspDatabase wrappers, preserve disabled-mode/team-swap behavior, and smoke valid DB, missing DB and timeout/degraded modes.

summary: Respawn/death lifecycle now has a canonical atlas mapping player death cleanup, pre-respawn action/skill reapply, custom respawn menu, camp/mobile/MASH/leader spawn rules, AI respawn, kill scoring and the MASH live-vs-dead-marker split. It also confirms a patch-ready mode-5 custom-gear penalty edge.
nextAction: Patch custom-gear penalty mode 5 only after owner confirms intended semantics, and smoke player death/menu/action reapply, MASH/mobile/camp/leader spawn options, AI respawn and MASH marker revive/remove separately.

summary: Upgrade/research flow now has a canonical atlas. The live player UI validates/debits locally, RequestUpgrade raw-spawns Server_ProcessUpgrade, and the server worker trusts side/id/level while owning replicated timer/state.
nextAction: Add a server-side upgrade request wrapper and side-config validator before adding or rebalancing upgrade mechanics.

summary: Town/camp runtime now has a canonical atlas. Server loops own town/camp side transitions and SV, while TownCaptured/CampCaptured client PVFs still award funds locally and request score after the capture broadcast.
nextAction: If capture reward hardening is claimed, keep capture ownership untouched and migrate bounty/score eligibility and funds mutation to a server-owned path with replay/wrong-side/distance rejection tests.

summary: HQ object state is mostly server/side-logic owned, but normal MHQ repair and WASP cash HQ recovery still rely on client-side funds/side effects and side-only repair requests.
nextAction: Design server-owned MHQ repair/recovery request shape with requester and repair/recovery context, then smoke commander handoff, HQ deploy/mobilize, HQ death, JIP wreck markers and repair.

Patch end condition/winner semantics in Chernarus source, explicitly decide non-zero threeway mode, retire stale PVFunctions/LogGameEnd.sqf, then run generated propagation and elimination/all-towns/same-tick smoke.
summary: Default victory loop can invert all-towns winner stats, double-fire same-tick eliminations and has non-zero threeway no-detection; stale PVFunctions/LogGameEnd.sqf is buggy if ever wired.

Join/disconnect lifecycle now has a canonical atlas. playerObjectsList.sqf likely updates the wrong WFBE_SE_PLAYERLIST row, disconnect cleanup deletes _old_unit before later setPos, and stale disconnect cleanup can race fast reconnects.
nextAction: Patch supply player-list indexing, resolve delete-versus-relocate disconnect behavior, and guard stale disconnect cleanup before changing reconnect or AntiStack behavior.

summary: Marker/cleanup/restoration systems now have a canonical atlas. Mine pair removal, garbage trash flags and side-local marker deletion behavior need patch or smoke before marker/cleanup changes.
nextAction: Fix mine pair removal, align trash flags, then MP-smoke side-local marker creation/deletion and HQ wreck marker visibility.

summary: Mission parameters/build inputs now have a canonical atlas. version.sqf remains a generated boot dependency missing from source checkout; LoadoutManager root discovery is fixed for normal repo-root marker checkouts and A2WASP_SKIP_ZIP makes missing 7za non-blocking for propagation-only runs.
nextAction: Add generated-file banners and release packaging verification with real 7za; keep A2WASP_SKIP_ZIP for docs/propagation-only runs.

summary: Support/special/tactical modules now have a canonical atlas. RequestSpecial forwards directly to Server_HandleSpecial while most fee, cooldown and upgrade gates are client-side; ZetaCargo detach, stale ICBM paths, MASH marker flow, UAV creation and supply mission cargo trust need hardening.
nextAction: Add server-side validation to RequestSpecial/HandleSpecial before accepting support effects; fix Zeta detach and reconcile stale ICBM/MASH marker paths separately.

summary: Chernarus source and maintained Vanilla Takistan now initialize _i before the WFBE_SE_PLAYERLIST forEach, so reconnecting UIDs can update their actual player-object row instead of index 0; reconnect/supply smoke remains pending.
nextAction: Smoke reconnect/JIP player-object replacement and supply mission completion lookup.

summary: Wave H reconfirmed supplyMissionActive.sqf is a dead twin, cooldown casing/start-response race remain active, WFBE_SE_PLAYERLIST still lacks disconnect/stale row cleanup, and completion still trusts client-stamped truck object vars.
nextAction: Patch cooldown key/defaults, server-side start verdict, player-list disconnect cleanup, dead-twin retirement and server-side reward validation as separate bounded steps after current source-only propagation gates are clear.

summary: Registered PVF dispatch hardening must be followed by per-handler authority checks for score, join, commander, MHQ repair, base-area and special-router flows.
Respawn/HQ docs should keep the MASH marker relay orphaned, MHQ repair authority-light, HQ-kill forwarding schema-sensitive and respawn penalty mode 5 patch-ready.

Score, funds and supply authority is mixed: RequestChangeScore and side-supply temp channels trust client/payload mutation, while supply-value reads and some award helpers provide safer server-derived patterns.
AntiStack and join lifecycle use UID as the durable key, but launch-connect is client-pushed, disconnect DB writes are unchecked, and stale allUnits/session snapshots remain a hardening target.
UI risks remain confirmed: stale old upgrade resource, EASA/Economy and title IDD collisions, RHUD folded into OptionsAvailable, service/EASA stale TODOs and economy sell/RespawnST cleanup debt.
Human commander systems are live, but full autonomous AI commander ownership is not proven. Commander percent, upgrade, sell, specials and MHQ repair remain authority-adjacent hardening lanes.
Base/mobile/camp respawn are live; local MASH respawn exists but marker sync is dead; HQ marker cleanup can stale after wreck loss and resistance HQ marker recovery is uncovered.

The five tracked source fixes have propagated to maintained Vanilla Takistan: paratrooper marker PV registration, duplicate Skill_Init removal, hosted FPS early exits, supply command-center scan narrowing and supply player-object list indexing. Arma 2 OA smoke remains pending.

UI/HUD/menus JIP/HC coverage is now reviewed. Headless clients are excluded from Init_Client.sqf by initJIPCompatible.sqf, while late-joining player clients have marker/vote/HUD recovery but still depend on several unbounded synced-variable waits and feature-specific publicVariable smoke.
nextAction: Do not treat high-command UI calls as headless-client UI. Smoke late-join marker/vote/HUD recovery and keep unbounded waitUntil risks routed to Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.

summary: Feature Status and Pending Owner Decisions now distinguish research-complete owner decisions from patch-ready local defects, routing code owners by decision class instead of asking future agents to re-read the whole ledger.
nextAction: Keep this as the first triage stop before gameplay patches; update it whenever an owner makes a server-authority, direct-PV, victory, supply-baseline or revive/remove decision.

summary: All 13 registered server-bound PVF handlers are now classified in the server authority map so future agents can distinguish dispatcher hardening from legitimate-command payload validation.
nextAction: After PVF dispatch allowlisting, claim one handler class at a time, starting with RequestSpecial/ICBM, score mutation, construction/defense and upgrades.

summary: All 15 registered client-bound PVF handlers are now classified by runtime effect and JIP/authority note, separating visual events from local money/score effects and broad routers.
nextAction: Use the matrix before changing JIP replay, command tasks, capture rewards, attack-wave display, paratrooper markers or localized money messages.

summary: SmallSite appends _nearLogic to wfbe_structures_logic at construction start and again at the post-completion cleanup point where its comment says removal should happen; MediumSite removes the sibling entry after completion.
nextAction: Patch source Chernarus post-completion SmallSite from append to remove, propagate maintained Vanilla with LoadoutManager, then smoke small/medium construction and JIP marker/init behavior.

summary: OptionsAvailable/RHUD/action icons and EndOfGameStats both set/clear uiNamespace currentCutDisplay; the RHUD loop can recreate OptionsAvailable while endgame stats writes through the same shared key.
nextAction: Split title display variables or gate RHUD/action-icon recreation during endgame; smoke RHUD/FPS, action icons and endgame stat bars together after source and Vanilla propagation.
